name: Marvin Gaye
abstract: Marvin Pentz Gaye Jr. (né Gay; April 2, 1939 – April 1, 1984) was an American R&B and soul singer, songwriter, musician, and record producer.

works_created.t: Mercy Mercy Me (The Ecology)/ What's Going On

about_quotes.speaker: Josiah Gluck
about_quotes.evidence: "...you listen to that very reverberant conga hit on “What’s Going On,” Marvin Gaye. That is also to me just a very — it’s a sonic signature to that."
about_quotes.doc_title: Josiah Gluck interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 13, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. And I mean that was Marvin Gaye who won the coveted Grammy award for “Sexual Healing.”

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: Marvin Gaye, because of what he did after the gig... shake my hand and say, “Man I really thank you for performing with me.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, that was something. Wasn't that somethin'? Here the guy worked and Marvin Gaye took him to court, civil court to get his money.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I extract a rhythm track from Marvin Gaye, as we know from hearing about the Pharrell — Robin Thicke stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: Rhythm & blues, which I mean like Marvin Gaye and the Four Tops and I still am a big fan of James Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 29, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]
